title: We Play At Paste,
content:
We play at paste,
    Till qualified for pearl,
    Then drop the paste,
    And deem ourself a fool.
    The shapes, though, were similar,
    And our new hands
    Learned gem-tactics
    Practising sands.
meaning:
"We Play At Paste," is a quintessential example of Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
